text:
Market today: Rough week for your universe. Tech had one of its worst weeks in a year — AI momentum cracked, Micron dragged chips down hard, and a U.S. strike on Iran is lifting oil and adding macro fear. Your heavily chip/tech book is directly in the crossfire.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and flagged SELL, sitting not far above your $336 stop. On margin, that hole gets deep fast. PLTR is down 13% and also flashing SELL — no obvious near-term catalyst to save it. SPAL is down nearly 17% — this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; it's bleeding even before leverage math, and your stop at $19 is close. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data — treat it like a live grenade on margin.

Watch: 1) Whether Iran tensions escalate further — oil spike hurts risk appetite and rotates money away from tech fast. 2) Any further chip sector selling following Micron's 6% drop — SMH is your only green name and could reverse.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are both screaming SELL — margin means you cannot afford to hope.
